ddls:
CREATE TABLE "load_data" (
  "load_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each load entry */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the load data was collected */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date of the load measurement */,
  "load_mw" REAL /* Power load in megawatts */,
  "time_of_day" TEXT /* Time of day for the load measurement */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the load data */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions at the time of measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("load_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_load_data_region FOREIGN KEY ("region") REFERENCES regions ("region_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "geographic_coordinates" TEXT /* Geographic coordinates of the region */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dates" (
  "date_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each date entry */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Actual date of measurement */,
  "day_of_week" INTEGER /* Day of the week for the date */,
  "holiday" INTEGER /* Indicates if the date is a holiday */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("date_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "load_summary" (
  "summary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the summary entry */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region for which the summary is calculated */,
  "date_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the date corresponding to the summary */,
  "average_load" REAL /* Average load in megawatts for the specified date and region */,
  "max_load" REAL /* Maximum load in megawatts for the specified date and region */,
  "min_load" REAL /* Minimum load in megawatts for the specified date and region */,
  "load_variance" REAL /* Variance of load data for statistical analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("summary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_load_summary_date_id FOREIGN KEY ("date_id") REFERENCES dates ("date_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_load_summary_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, administrator) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "failed_logins" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "load_forecast" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast entry */,
  "date_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the date for which the forecast is made */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region for which the forecast is made */,
  "predicted_load" REAL /* Predicted load in megawatts */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_load_forecast_date_id FOREIGN KEY ("date_id") REFERENCES dates ("date_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_load_forecast_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather entry */,
  "date_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the date for which the weather data is collected */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region for which the weather data is collected */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Average temperature */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Average humidity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_date_id FOREIGN KEY ("date_id") REFERENCES dates ("date_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "special_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "date_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the date of the event */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the event occurs */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of special event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_special_events_date_id FOREIGN KEY ("date_id") REFERENCES dates ("date_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_special_events_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)
